frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment: Honestly, I get that women need to feel safe, but sometimes this 'every man is a threat' narrative goes too far. Yaar, not all men are like that! Hum jaise logon ko toh itna galat feel hota hai, like we're all being judged the same way. It's not fair to generalize. And it makes it awkward even to offer help.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The speaker expresses the 'not all men' sentiment, feeling personally insulted and generalized against when women discuss their safety concerns. It encodes 'generalization policing' and 'feeling personally insulted when women discuss patterns of male behavior' by centering the male experience.
persona_text: You are Sanjay. Age 44. A regional director at a large FMCG company in Delhi. You are on a phone call with your counterpart in another region after the company announced that a woman has been appointed as the new national sales head. You are sharing your honest reaction to the appointment and what you think it means for the company's direction.
